title: Bootstrap HTMX infrastructure
description:
Set up the foundational infrastructure for HTMX-based frontend, replacing SvelteKit.

## Purpose
Establish the base patterns and tooling needed before any page conversion can begin.

## Scope

### 1. templ Setup
- Install templ CLI (go install github.com/a-h/templ/cmd/templ@latest)
- Add templ generate to Makefile
- Configure hot reload for development (templ generate --watch)
- Verify templ files compile and integrate with Go

### 2. Base Layout Template
- Create layouts/base.templ with:
  - HTML5 doctype, meta tags, viewport
  - HTMX script include
  - Alpine.js script include  
  - Tailwind CSS (existing setup)
  - Header component (logo, nav)
  - Footer component
  - Main content slot
- Match current v2 visual design (gf-maroon, gf-sky-blue, etc.)

### 3. HTMX Integration
- Add htmx.min.js to static assets (or CDN)
- Configure any HTMX extensions needed (e.g., response-targets)
- Add hx-boost to base layout for progressive enhancement
- Test basic hx-get works

### 4. Alpine.js Integration
- Add alpine.min.js to static assets (or CDN)
- Verify x-data, x-show, x-transition work
- Document when to use Alpine vs HTMX

### 5. Go Handler Patterns
- Create example handler showing the pattern:
  - Full page render (returns base layout + content)
  - Partial render (returns just fragment for HTMX swap)
  - Detect HX-Request header to choose response type
- Document pattern in code comments

### 6. Static File Serving
- Ensure Go serves templ-rendered HTML for routes
- Verify static assets (CSS, JS, images) still work
- Update SPA fallback logic if needed

### 7. Development Workflow
- make dev-htmx or similar for templ watch + go run
- Document workflow in v2/CLAUDE.md

## Deliverables
1. Compilable templ templates
2. Base layout matching current design
3. Working HTMX + Alpine on a test route
4. Updated Makefile with templ commands
5. Documentation of patterns and workflow

## Out of Scope
- Converting any real pages (that's the tracer bullet)
- Admin functionality
- Authentication
close_reason: Completed HTMX bootstrap infrastructure with templ templates, HTMX/Alpine assets, example handler, Makefile commands, and documentation

title: Clean up legacy IAM policies via OpenTofu
description:
Clean up legacy/cruft in IAM policies as part of OpenTofu adoption.

## Cleanup Items

### 1. Remove dead SQS reference
The `GallfomersImagesPolicy` inline policy on `s3-upload` user references a deleted SQS queue:

```json
{
  "Effect": "Allow",
  "Action": "sqs:SendMessage",
  "Resource": "arn:aws:sqs:*:885187511538:gallformers_create_images"
}
```

This queue no longer exists - remove the statement.

### 2. Fix policy name typo
Current: `GallfomersImagesPolicy` (missing 'r')
Should be: `GallformersImagesPolicy`

### 3. Consolidate s3-upload policies
Currently `s3-upload` user has:
- Attached policy: `s3-put`
- Inline policy: `GallfomersImagesPolicy`

Both grant `s3:Put*` on `gallformers/*`. Consolidate into single managed policy:

```hcl
resource "aws_iam_policy" "image_upload" {
  name = "GallformersImageUpload"
  
  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Sid      = "ImageUpload"
        Effect   = "Allow"
        Action   = ["s3:Put*"]
        Resource = ["arn:aws:s3:::gallformers/*"]
      },
      {
        Sid      = "ListBucket"
        Effect   = "Allow"
        Action   = ["s3:ListBucket"]
        Resource = ["arn:aws:s3:::gallformers"]
      }
    ]
  })
}
```

### 4. Enable versioning on images bucket
Currently `gallformers` bucket has versioning disabled. Enable for safety:

```hcl
resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "images" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.images.id
  versioning_configuration {
    status = "Enabled"
  }
}
```

## Execution Order

1. Import existing resources first
2. Update OpenTofu configs with cleaned-up versions
3. Run `tofu plan` to verify changes
4. Apply changes

title: Migrate gallformers images bucket to us-east-1
description:
Migrate the `gallformers` S3 bucket from us-east-2 to us-east-1 to unify all AWS resources in one region.

## Current State

- Bucket: `gallformers`
- Current region: `us-east-2` (Ohio)
- Target region: `us-east-1` (N. Virginia)
- Content: Production images (~6,500 images, multiple sizes each)
- Access: Public read via bucket policy
- Versioning: Not enabled
- CloudFront origin: `gallformers.s3.amazonaws.com`

## Migration Options

**Option A: Create new bucket + sync + rename**
1. Create `gallformers-images` in us-east-1
2. Use `aws s3 sync` to copy all objects
3. Update CloudFront origin
4. Update app configs
5. Delete old bucket (or keep as backup)

**Option B: AWS S3 Replication + cutover**
1. Enable cross-region replication to new bucket
2. Wait for sync
3. Cutover CloudFront and apps
4. Disable replication, delete old bucket

## CloudFront Update Required

After migration, update CloudFront distribution `E3B3XXYW8G4SB2`:
- Origin: `gallformers.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com` (or new bucket name)

## Bucket Configuration to Preserve

```json
{
  "BucketPolicy": {
    "Version": "2008-10-17",
    "Statement": [{
      "Sid": "AllowPublicRead",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": {"AWS": "*"},
      "Action": "s3:GetObject",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::NEW_BUCKET_NAME/*"
    }]
  },
  "PublicAccessBlock": {
    "BlockPublicAcls": false,
    "IgnorePublicAcls": false,
    "BlockPublicPolicy": false,
    "RestrictPublicBuckets": false
  }
}
```

## Considerations

- Schedule during low-traffic period
- CloudFront will serve cached content during transition
- May need to update V1 and V2 app configs if bucket name changes
- Consider enabling versioning on new bucket

title: Finalize define-v2-foundation open items
description:
## Context

The `define-v2-foundation` proposal has been reviewed and updated. Four open items remain that require research and decisions before the proposal can be applied.

## Open Items

### 1. Backup Strategy (tasks.md 2.1)

Research and decide on a backup strategy for the v2 Fly.io SQLite database:

**Options to evaluate:**
- **Litestream**: Streams SQLite changes to S3 automatically. Low complexity, proven pattern.
- **Manual backups**: Scheduled `fly ssh sftp get` to backup location
- **Fly.io native**: Check if Fly.io has built-in volume backup options
- **Other**: Any other viable approaches

**Decision criteria:**
- Reliability (can we restore from disaster?)
- Complexity (how much setup/maintenance?)
- Cost (S3 storage, etc.)
- Recovery time (how fast can we restore?)

### 2. Environment Variables and Secrets Strategy (tasks.md 2.2)

Define and document the complete environment variable strategy:

**Required env vars to identify:**
- `DATABASE_PATH` (already defined)
- `PROD_HOST` (for download-db)
- Auth0 credentials (CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, etc.)
- S3 credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, etc.)
- Any others needed

**Document for each:**
- Local dev: How set? (.env file pattern)
- CI/CD: How set? (GitHub secrets)
- Fly.io: How set? (fly secrets)

**Deliverable:** Update design.md with a new "Environment Variables" section listing all vars and how to configure them in each environment.

### 3. CI/CD Strategy (tasks.md 2.3)

Define the CI/CD pipeline for v2 code:

**Questions to answer:**
- Should v2 CI be in a separate workflow file or added to existing CI.yml?
- What checks run on PRs touching `v2/`? (Go tests, Svelte build, type checking)
- What triggers deployment? (push to main with v2 changes only?)
- Should deployment wait for tests to pass first?

**Deliverable:** Draft the actual workflow YAML (or key structure) in design.md.

### 4. Rollback Strategy (tasks.md 2.4)

Document the rollback procedure:

**Questions to answer:**
- How do we detect a bad deployment? (monitoring, manual check, health endpoint)
- What are the exact rollback commands? (`fly releases`, `fly deploy --image <previous>`)
- What about database changes? (SQLite has no point-in-time recovery without backups)
- Who can perform rollback? (anyone with Fly.io access)

**Deliverable:** Add a "Rollback Procedure" section to design.md with step-by-step instructions.

## Agent Instructions

Work through these items one at a time with the user:

1. Present the options/considerations for each item
2. Ask clarifying questions if needed
3. Get the user's decision
4. Update the relevant files (design.md, tasks.md) with the decisions
5. Mark items complete as you go

After all items are resolved, the proposal should be ready for final review and application.

## Files to Update

- `openspec/changes/define-v2-foundation/design.md` - Add new sections for decisions
- `openspec/changes/define-v2-foundation/tasks.md` - Mark items complete as resolved

title: Migrate remaining raw HTML form elements (selects, textareas)
description:
Migrate remaining raw HTML form elements to use the <.input> component.

## Scope

Per the epic, there are raw HTML elements that should use components:
- Selects: 21 instances
- Textareas: 7 instances
- Text inputs: 29 instances (some covered by gallformers-bk2t for admin)

Note: Checkboxes covered by gallformers-zrgu, buttons covered by gallformers-ud6h.

## The <.input> Component

core_components.ex has a versatile input component:
```elixir
<.input type="text" field={@form[:name]} label="Name" />
<.input type="select" field={@form[:status]} label="Status" options={@options} />
<.input type="textarea" field={@form[:description]} label="Description" />
```

## Migration Pattern

**Before (raw select):**
```heex
<label class="...">Status</label>
<select name="status" class="...">
  <option value="a">A</option>
</select>
```

**After:**
```heex
<.input type="select" name="status" label="Status" options={[{"A", "a"}]} />
```

## Tasks

1. Find all raw <select> elements - migrate to <.input type="select">
2. Find all raw <textarea> elements - migrate to <.input type="textarea">  
3. Find remaining raw <input type="text"> on public pages - migrate to <.input>
4. Verify form submissions still work
5. Run precommit

## Exceptions

- Inputs that are part of component internals (e.g., inside multi_select)
- Search inputs that need special handling (may use search_input component)

## Reference

See v2/docs/component-audit.md "Raw HTML Usage" section.

title: Audit component files and document canonical component set
description:
Audit all 3 component files and create a definitive list of what components should exist and where they should live.

## Files to Audit

1. v2/lib/gallformers_web/components/core_components.ex
2. v2/lib/gallformers_web/components/form_components.ex  
3. v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/form_components.ex
4. v2/assets/css/app.css (for existing semantic classes like .gf-checkbox)

## Tasks

1. List every component defined in each file with:
   - Component name
   - Purpose
   - Current styling (especially label font sizes)
   - Whether it handles edge cases correctly (e.g., checkbox hidden input)

2. Identify duplicates:
   - Known: rename_modal exists in both form_components.ex files
   - Check for any other duplicates or near-duplicates

3. Identify gaps:
   - Components that exist but aren't being used
   - Raw HTML patterns that should have components but don't

4. **Audit existing semantic CSS classes in app.css:**
   - .gf-checkbox exists - what else exists?
   - What classes need to be created for centralized styling?

5. **Propose semantic CSS class structure:**
   All form styling should be defined ONCE in app.css so a single change propagates everywhere:
   ```css
   .gf-label { /* all label styling */ }
   .gf-input { /* text input styling */ }
   .gf-select { /* select styling */ }
   .gf-textarea { /* textarea styling */ }
   .gf-checkbox { /* already exists */ }
   .gf-btn-primary { /* primary button */ }
   .gf-btn-secondary { /* secondary button */ }
   /* etc. */
   ```

6. Propose canonical component structure:
   - What belongs in core_components.ex (primitives used everywhere)
   - What belongs in components/form_components.ex (extended components used app-wide)
   - What belongs in admin/form_components.ex (truly admin-specific)

## Output

Create a markdown document at v2/docs/component-audit.md with:
- Current state inventory
- Identified issues (duplicates, inconsistencies)
- Proposed semantic CSS classes (defined in app.css)
- Proposed canonical component structure
- Migration recommendations

## Key Principle

**Single source of truth for styling**: Components should reference semantic CSS classes from app.css, NOT hardcode Tailwind classes. This allows global style changes from one location.

## Notes

- The <.input> component in core_components.ex is the gold standard for behavior - uses text-base, has hidden input for checkboxes
- Public pages use text-base for labels (correct)
- Admin pages accidentally use text-sm for labels (incorrect)
- .gf-checkbox already exists in app.css - use this pattern for all form elements
close_reason: Audit complete, findings in v2/docs/component-audit.md, beads reconciled

title: Remove unnecessary [REPLACE] bracket escaping in SQL
description:
In libs/db/taxonomy.ts:632, the SQL uses [REPLACE] with square bracket escaping:

```sql
SET name = [REPLACE](name, SUBSTRING(...), ...)
```

The brackets are SQL Server-style identifier escaping. While SQLite accepts this syntax, it's unnecessary since REPLACE is not a reserved word.

**Fix:** Simply change `[REPLACE]` to `REPLACE`.

This is cosmetic/cleanup only - the code works correctly as-is.

title: Create generic modal/1 component in core_components
description:
Create a generic, reusable modal component to replace the specialized rename_modal. ## Problem Currently `rename_modal/1` exists (duplicated in 2 files) but it's too specialized. We need a generic modal that can be composed with any content. ## Component Design ```elixir @doc """ Generic modal component. ## Examples <.modal id="confirm-delete" on_cancel={JS.hide(to: "#confirm-delete")}> <:header>Confirm Delete <:body>Are you sure? <:footer> <.button variant="secondary" phx-click={JS.hide(to: "#confirm-delete")}>Cancel <.button variant="danger" phx-click="delete">Delete """ attr :id, :string, required: true attr :show, :boolean, default: false attr :on_cancel, JS, default: %JS{} slot :header slot :body, required: true slot :footer def modal(assigns) do ~H"""
<%= render_slot(@header) %>
<%= render_slot(@body) %>
<%= render_slot(@footer) %>

title: Fix nested modal escape key handling in article admin
description:
## Problem

The article admin page has nested modals that don't handle Escape key properly:

1. **Article Edit Modal** - the main editing modal
2. **Image Browser Modal** - opened from within article edit
3. **Image Insert Modal** - opened from within image browser
4. **Image Delete Confirmation** - opened from within image browser

When pressing Escape or clicking outside to dismiss an inner modal, BOTH the inner modal AND the parent article modal close. This is extremely frustrating UX - users lose their editing context.

## Root Cause

Phoenix's `<.modal>` component uses `phx-window-keydown` with `phx-key="escape"` on each modal. When multiple modals are rendered, ALL of them receive the window-level Escape event simultaneously. Both events fire from JavaScript before any server-side code runs.

## Failed Approaches

1. **Server-side flag** - Doesn't work because both events fire before any server code runs

2. **Conditional `on_cancel`** - Doesn't work because both events still fire from JS

3. **Panel-based approach** - Converted nested modals to overlay panels inside the article modal. Created new problems with form dirty state and complex conditional logic.

## Current State

The code currently uses the panel-based approach which is partially working but brittle and overly complex.

## Next Step

**Discussion needed**: Given the limitations of Phoenix's modal component, how should nested modals behave in this application? Options to consider:

- Modify the modal component itself?
- Use JavaScript to intercept events?
- Redesign the UX to avoid nested modals?
- Accept limitations and use explicit close buttons only?
- Something else?

## Files Involved

- `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/article_admin_live.ex`
- `v2/lib/gallformers_web/components/core_components.ex` (modal component, lines 598-674)

title: Import existing S3 buckets into OpenTofu
description:
Import existing S3 buckets into OpenTofu state to manage them as code.

## Buckets to Import

| Bucket | Region | Import After Migration? |
|--------|--------|------------------------|
| `gallformers` (or new name) | us-east-1 | Yes, after migration |
| `gallformers-backups` | us-east-1 | Yes |
| `gallformers-full-backups` | us-east-1 | Yes |
| `gallformers-dev` | us-east-2 | Maybe (consider deprecating) |

## Import Commands

```bash
# Import bucket
tofu import aws_s3_bucket.backups gallformers-backups
tofu import aws_s3_bucket.full_backups gallformers-full-backups

# Import bucket versioning
tofu import aws_s3_bucket_versioning.backups gallformers-backups
tofu import aws_s3_bucket_versioning.full_backups gallformers-full-backups

# Import bucket policies  
tofu import aws_s3_bucket_policy.backups gallformers-backups
```

## Example Resource Definitions

```hcl
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "backups" {
  bucket = "gallformers-backups"
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "backups" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.backups.id
  versioning_configuration {
    status = "Enabled"
  }
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_policy" "backups" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.backups.id
  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Sid       = "PublicReadSnapshots"
      Effect    = "Allow"
      Principal = "*"
      Action    = "s3:GetObject"
      Resource  = "${aws_s3_bucket.backups.arn}/public/*"
    }]
  })
}
```

## Notes

- Run `tofu plan` after import to verify no drift
- May need to adjust resource definitions to match actual state
- Consider using `tofu import` with `-generate-config-out` flag

title: Decide fate of gallformers-dev S3 bucket
description:
Decide whether to keep, migrate, or deprecate the `gallformers-dev` S3 bucket.

## Current State

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Bucket | `gallformers-dev` |
| Region | us-east-2 |
| Purpose | Development image storage |
| Access | Unknown (likely private) |
| Referenced in | `.env.local-docker` |

## Options

### Option A: Deprecate
- Stop using dev bucket
- Use production bucket for all environments (with path prefixes if needed)
- Simpler infrastructure
- Risk: Dev mistakes could affect prod images

### Option B: Migrate to us-east-1
- Create new bucket in us-east-1
- Sync any existing content
- Update `.env.local-docker` reference
- Keeps dev/prod separation

### Option C: Keep as-is
- Leave in us-east-2
- Accept region inconsistency
- Minimal effort

## Questions to Answer

1. Is the dev bucket actively used?
2. Are there any images in it currently?
3. Is dev/prod separation important for images?
4. Worth the maintenance overhead?

## Recommendation

Likely **Option A (Deprecate)** unless there's active use. Images are immutable and environment-agnostic - same image can be used in dev and prod. Path prefixes (`dev/`, `prod/`) could provide separation if needed.

title: Research Auth0 alternatives for V2
description:
## Requirements

- User managed ID - email, password, resets, etc. - do not want to see user info or write code to encrypt/manage any of this
- Simple role-based access
- Can recreate accounts or migrate from Auth0 (even if users need to reset password)
- Should work with Go API + Svelte frontend tech stack
- Widely used and supported

## Research Findings

### Top Candidates

**1. Keycloak**
- Most battle-tested, used by Red Hat, huge community
- Full user management, password resets, RBAC, social login
- Standard OIDC - works with any stack
- Self-host (Docker) or managed options exist
- Downside: Java-based, heavier resource usage (~512MB-1GB RAM)

**2. Zitadel** (Recommended)
- Newer but production-ready, growing fast
- User management, RBAC, OIDC, modern UI
- Written in Go (great fit for our stack), lightweight
- Self-host (Docker) or their cloud (free tier)
- Downside: Smaller community than Keycloak

**3. Ory Kratos + Keto**
- Solid, used by companies like Raspberry Pi
- Kratos = identity, Keto = permissions (modular)
- Written in Go, very lightweight
- Self-host (Docker)
- Downside: More assembly required (separate services)

## Comparison

| | Keycloak | Zitadel | Ory |
|---|----------|---------|-----|
| Ease of setup | Medium | Easy | Harder |
| Resource usage | Heavy | Light | Light |
| Community size | Huge | Growing | Medium |
| Go-native | No (Java) | Yes | Yes |
| Admin UI | Yes | Yes | Limited |
| RBAC built-in | Yes | Yes | Separate (Keto) |

## Current Status
Using Auth0 for V2. This bead is for future consideration if Auth0 continues to be problematic.

title: Move duplicated helper functions to shared module
description:
## Problem

Two helper functions are duplicated between the gall and host admin forms:
1. `valid_species_name?/1` - validates binomial species name format
2. `alias_type_options/0` - returns dropdown options for alias types

## Current Locations

### valid_species_name?/1

In `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/gall_live/form.ex` (~line 484):
```elixir
defp valid_species_name?(name) do
  Regex.match?(~r/^[A-Z][a-z-]+ (x )?[a-z-]+/, name)
end
```

In `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/host_live/form.ex` (~line 319):
```elixir
defp valid_species_name?(name) do
  Regex.match?(~r/^[A-Z][a-z-]+ (x )?[a-z-]+/, name)
end
```

### alias_type_options/0

In `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/gall_live/form.ex` (~line 639):
```elixir
defp alias_type_options do
  [
    {"Common Name", "common name"},
    {"Scientific Synonym", "scientific synonym"},
    {"Other", "other"}
  ]
end
```

In `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/host_live/form.ex` (~line 412):
```elixir
defp alias_type_options do
  [
    {"Common Name", "common name"},
    {"Scientific Synonym", "scientific synonym"},
    {"Other", "other"}
  ]
end
```

## Solution

Move both functions to a shared location. Two options:

### Option A: Add to FormHelpers (recommended)

Add to `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/form_helpers.ex` inside the `__using__` macro's `quote do` block:

```elixir
def valid_species_name?(name) do
  Regex.match?(~r/^[A-Z][a-z-]+ (x )?[a-z-]+/, name)
end

def alias_type_options do
  [
    {"Common Name", "common name"},
    {"Scientific Synonym", "scientific synonym"},
    {"Other", "other"}
  ]
end
```

This makes them available to any module that does `use GallformersWeb.Admin.FormHelpers`.

### Option B: Add to Species context

If `valid_species_name?/1` is considered domain logic, add to `v2/lib/gallformers/species.ex`:

```elixir
@doc "Validates that a name follows binomial nomenclature (Genus species or Genus x species)"
def valid_species_name?(name) do
  Regex.match?(~r/^[A-Z][a-z-]+ (x )?[a-z-]+/, name)
end
```

And `alias_type_options/0` could go in a new `v2/lib/gallformers/aliases.ex` or stay in FormHelpers.

## Files to modify

1. `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/form_helpers.ex` - Add shared functions
2. `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/gall_live/form.ex` - Remove local definitions
3. `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/host_live/form.ex` - Remove local definitions

## Testing

Run existing tests:
```bash
mix test test/gallformers_web/live/admin/gall_live/form_test.exs test/gallformers_web/live/admin/host_live/form_test.exs
```

All rename validation tests should continue to pass.
close_reason: Moved valid_species_name?/1 and alias_type_options/0 to FormHelpers

title: Extract duplicated rename modal to shared component
description:
## Problem The rename modal HTML is duplicated (~50 lines) between the gall and host admin forms: - `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/gall_live/form.ex` (lines ~1086-1161) - `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/host_live/form.ex` (lines ~710-785) This violates DRY and means any fix needs to be applied twice. ## Current Implementation Both forms have nearly identical modals with: - Modal backdrop and container - Title ("Edit Gall Name" vs "Edit Host Name") - Input field for new name bound to `@rename_value` - Checkbox for "Add Alias for old name" bound to `@add_alias_on_rename` - Cancel and Rename buttons - Error display for `@rename_error` The only differences are: 1. Title text ("Gall" vs "Host") 2. Entity type references in labels ## Solution Extract to a component in `v2/lib/gallformers_web/components/form_components.ex`: ```elixir attr :show, :boolean, required: true attr :value, :string, required: true attr :add_alias_checked, :boolean, required: true attr :error, :string, default: nil attr :entity_type, :string, required: true # "Gall" or "Host" def rename_modal(assigns) do ~H""" <%= if @show do %>
<% end %> """ end ``` ## Events to handle Both forms use these events (keep in LiveView, just call shared component): - `open_rename_modal` - sets `@show_rename_modal = true` - `close_rename_modal` - sets `@show_rename_modal = false` - `update_rename_value` - updates `@rename_value` - `toggle_add_alias_on_rename` - toggles `@add_alias_on_rename` - `do_rename` - validates and performs rename ## Files to modify 1. `v2/lib/gallformers_web/components/form_components.ex` - Add `rename_modal/1` component 2. `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/gall_live/form.ex` - Replace inline modal with component call 3. `v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/host_live/form.ex` - Replace inline modal with component call ## Testing Run existing tests to verify no regression: ```bash mix test test/gallformers_web/live/admin/gall_live/form_test.exs test/gallformers_web/live/admin/host_live/form_test.exs ``` The rename modal tests in both files should continue to pass.

title: Refactor gall and host forms to use shared components
description:
Refactor gall_live/form.ex and host_live/form.ex to use shared components and DeferredChanges.

## Forms to Refactor

1. gall_live/form.ex (~1167 lines) - most complex
2. host_live/form.ex (~782 lines)

## Changes for Both Forms

**Use FormComponents:**
- Replace alias table markup with: <.alias_editor aliases={@aliases} ... />
- Replace rename modal markup with: <.rename_modal show={@show_rename_modal} ... />
- Replace button markup with: <.form_actions form_dirty={@form_dirty} mode={@mode} />

**Use DeferredChanges:**
- Replace original_X/X assigns with: assign(DeferredChanges.init(:aliases, aliases))
- Replace add_alias logic with: DeferredChanges.add_pending(socket, :aliases, attrs)
- Replace remove_alias logic with: DeferredChanges.remove_pending(socket, :aliases, id)
- Replace duplicate check with: DeferredChanges.exists?(socket, :aliases, :name, name)
- Replace diff logic in save with: DeferredChanges.compute_changes(socket, :aliases)
- Replace post-save reload with: DeferredChanges.refresh(socket, :aliases, items)

**Use FormHelpers (where applicable):**
- init_admin_form for mount basics
- But keep custom apply_action due to complex related data loading

## Gall-Specific Considerations

- Has 9 filter types (colors, shapes, etc.) - each needs DeferredChanges tracking
- Filter dropdown components handled by separate agent (gallformers-jzp1)
- Has hosts picker (typeahead) - component handled by separate agent
- Keep filter_values as single map with DeferredChanges, not 9 separate collections

## Host-Specific Considerations

- Has range map (places) - use DeferredChanges for places list
- Range map JS hook stays unchanged

## Expected Outcome

- gall_live/form.ex: ~1167 lines → ~500-600 lines
- host_live/form.ex: ~782 lines → ~350-450 lines

## Testing

Same as simple forms, plus:
1. Test adding/removing aliases (deferred until Save)
2. Test rename modal flow
3. Test that Cancel discards pending alias changes
4. For gall: test filter property changes persist correctly
5. For host: test range map changes persist correctly

- Purpose: Admin/billing
- Attached policy: `AWSBillingReadOnlyAccess` (AWS managed)
- **Note**: May want to exclude from OpenTofu (personal admin user)

## IAM Policies to Import

### `LitestreamGallformersBackup` (v3)
ARN: `arn:aws:iam::885187511538:policy/LitestreamGallformersBackup`

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Sid": "LitestreamBackups",
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": ["s3:GetObject", "s3:PutObject", "s3:DeleteObject", "s3:ListBucket", "s3:GetBucketLocation"],
    "Resource": [
      "arn:aws:s3:::gallformers-backups",
      "arn:aws:s3:::gallformers-backups/*",
      "arn:aws:s3:::gallformers-full-backups",
      "arn:aws:s3:::gallformers-full-backups/*"
    ]
  }]
}
```

### `s3-put`
ARN: `arn:aws:iam::885187511538:policy/s3-put`

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": ["s3:Put*"],
    "Resource": ["arn:aws:s3:::gallformers/*"]
  }]
}
```

## Import Commands

```bash
# Import users
tofu import aws_iam_user.litestream litestream-gallformers
tofu import aws_iam_user.s3_upload s3-upload

# Import policies
tofu import aws_iam_policy.litestream_backup arn:aws:iam::885187511538:policy/LitestreamGallformersBackup
tofu import aws_iam_policy.s3_put arn:aws:iam::885187511538:policy/s3-put

# Import policy attachments
tofu import aws_iam_user_policy_attachment.litestream_backup litestream-gallformers/arn:aws:iam::885187511538:policy/LitestreamGallformersBackup
tofu import aws_iam_user_policy_attachment.s3_upload_put s3-upload/arn:aws:iam::885187511538:policy/s3-put

# Import inline policy (will be converted to managed)
tofu import aws_iam_user_policy.s3_upload_images s3-upload:GallfomersImagesPolicy
```

## Notes

- Access keys are NOT imported (security) - they stay in secrets management
- Consider converting inline policy to managed policy during import

title: Set up OpenTofu project structure and state backend
description:
Initialize OpenTofu project structure in the repo and configure S3 backend for state storage.

## Tasks

1. Create `infra/` directory in repo root (or `v2/infra/` if V2-only)
2. Install OpenTofu locally (`brew install opentofu`)
3. Create `main.tf` with AWS provider configuration
4. Create S3 bucket for OpenTofu state (e.g., `gallformers-terraform-state`)
5. Configure S3 backend with native locking (OpenTofu 1.10+)
6. Create `variables.tf` for configurable values
7. Add `.gitignore` entries for `.terraform/` and `*.tfstate*`

## State Backend Configuration

```hcl
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }
  
  backend "s3" {
    bucket       = "gallformers-terraform-state"
    key          = "infra/terraform.tfstate"
    region       = "us-east-1"
    encrypt      = true
    use_lockfile = true  # Native S3 locking, no DynamoDB needed
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = "us-east-1"
}
```

## Notes

- State bucket should have versioning enabled
- Consider enabling bucket encryption
- AWS credentials via standard methods (env vars, ~/.aws/credentials, IAM role)

title: Investigate infrastructure-as-code for AWS resources
description:
Research and potentially implement infrastructure-as-code (OpenTofu) for managing AWS resources.

## Decision
**Tool**: OpenTofu (open source Terraform fork under CNCF)
- Truly open source (MPL 2.0)
- Self-hosted state in S3
- No vendor dependency
- HCL syntax, huge ecosystem

## Current Resources (documented in v2/docs/aws-inventory.md)

**S3 Buckets:**
- `gallformers` (us-east-2, migrating to us-east-1) - Production images
- `gallformers-dev` (us-east-2) - Dev images
- `gallformers-backups` (us-east-1) - Litestream + sanitized public snapshots
- `gallformers-full-backups` (us-east-1) - Full backups with PII

**IAM:**
- `litestream-gallformers` user with `LitestreamGallformersBackup` policy
- Unknown image read/write users (need console audit)

**CloudFront:**
- Distribution at dhz6u1p7t6okk.cloudfront.net (ID unknown)

**Planned:**
- Lambda for image processing (per add-image-processing proposal)

## Region Strategy
Unify all resources to us-east-1 to match Fly.io iad datacenter.

## Next Steps
1. Look up unknowns in AWS console
2. Prototype OpenTofu config
3. Import existing resources
close_reason: Research complete. Created epic gallformers-nq4j with full implementation plan. See v2/docs/aws-inventory.md for documented resources.

title: Refactor dirty state tracking into reusable component for admin forms
description:
## Context
The article admin page now has dirty state tracking and a discard changes confirmation modal. This pattern should be reusable across all admin edit forms.

## Current Implementation (in article_admin_live.ex)
- `form_dirty` assign tracks whether form has been modified
- `show_discard_confirm` assign controls the confirmation modal
- `request_cancel` event checks dirty state before closing
- `cancel_discard` / `confirm_discard` events for the modal
- Save button disabled when not dirty

## Refactoring Goals
1. Create a reusable LiveView helper module or component for dirty state tracking
2. Extract the discard confirmation modal into a reusable component
3. Provide hooks/callbacks for form open, validate, save, and cancel
4. Apply to all admin edit forms:
   - Gall form
   - Host form
   - Source form
   - Taxonomy form
   - Glossary form
   - Place form
   - Filter Terms form
   - Article form (already has it)

## Approach Options
A. **LiveView Helper Module** - Functions to inject into LiveViews via `use`
B. **LiveComponent** - Wrap forms in a component that handles state
C. **JS Hook** - Track dirty state client-side with beforeunload warning

Recommend Option A for consistency with existing patterns.
close_reason: Implemented FormHelpers module with reusable dirty state tracking and discard confirmation modal. Applied to all admin forms: glossary, source, gall, host, taxonomy, place, filter_terms, and article_admin.

title: Review and verify existing species name update logic
description:
Analyze updateExistingSpecies() in libs/db/taxonomy.ts (lines 628-642) to verify the raw SQL correctly handles edge cases.

**Current Implementation:**
```sql
UPDATE species
SET name = [REPLACE](name, SUBSTRING(name, 1, INSTR(name, ' ') - 1), '${g.name}') 
WHERE id IN (
    SELECT st.species_id
    FROM taxonomy AS t
    INNER JOIN speciestaxonomy AS st ON t.id = st.taxonomy_id
    WHERE t.id = ${g.id} 
);
```

**Edge Cases to Verify:**
- Species with unusual naming patterns (not 'Genus epithet' format)
- Species names that already don't match their genus
- Empty or null cases
- Species with multiple words after genus (subspecies, etc.)

**Security Concern:**
- SQL injection risk: genus name is string-interpolated directly into SQL
- Consider using parameterized queries

**Test approach:**
- Test the REPLACE logic manually with sample data
- Check existing species in DB for non-standard naming patterns

title: Add s3:DeleteObject permission to s3-upload IAM user
description:
The `s3-upload` IAM user needs `s3:DeleteObject` permission to support deleting article images from the admin UI.

Current error:
```
User: arn:aws:iam::885187511538:user/s3-upload is not authorized to perform: s3:DeleteObject
on resource: "arn:aws:s3:::gallformers/articles/*" because no identity-based policy allows
the s3:DeleteObject action
```

## Fix Required

Update the IAM policy for `s3-upload` user to include DeleteObject:

```json
{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": [
        "s3:PutObject",
        "s3:DeleteObject"
    ],
    "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::gallformers/*"
}
```

Or if more restrictive, at minimum:
```json
{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": "s3:DeleteObject",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::gallformers/articles/*"
}
```
close_reason: Fixed via AWS CLI - added s3:DeleteObject to s3-put policy (now v2). Documented in OpenTofu epic gallformers-nq4j.

title: Create DeferredChanges helper module for related data tracking
description:
Create v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/deferred_changes.ex for tracking original vs pending state.

## Purpose

Gall and host forms have related data (aliases, hosts, places, filter values) that lives in separate DB tables. Changes should not persist until Save is clicked. This module provides helpers for tracking what was loaded vs what the user has changed.

## Functions to implement:

**init(collection_name, items)**
Returns map with original_X and X keys for socket assigns.
```elixir
DeferredChanges.init(:aliases, loaded_aliases)
# => %{original_aliases: [...], aliases: [...]}
```

**add_pending(socket, collection_name, item_attrs)**
Adds item with negative temp ID and pending: true flag.

**remove_pending(socket, collection_name, id)**
Removes item by ID from the collection.

**exists?(socket, collection_name, field, value)**
Checks if item with field=value exists (for duplicate prevention).

**compute_changes(socket, collection_name, id_field \\ :id)**
Returns {to_add, to_remove} lists by diffing original vs current.
- to_add: items with pending: true or negative IDs
- to_remove: items in original but not in current

**refresh(socket, collection_name, items)**
Updates both original_X and X after successful save.

## Reference

See gall_live/form.ex:
- Lines 56-68, 112-125: original vs pending assigns
- Lines 521-535: save_alias_changes diffing logic
- Lines 538-556: save_host_changes diffing logic

See host_live/form.ex:
- Lines 50-55, 90-95: original vs pending assigns
- Lines 357-387: save_alias_changes and save_place_changes

title: Standardize label styling to text-base across all components
description:
Create centralized CSS classes for form labels and update all components to use them.

## The Problem

Form label styling is currently hardcoded in multiple places:
- Some use 'text-sm font-medium text-gray-700'
- Some use 'text-base font-medium text-gray-700'

If we want to change label styling, we'd have to edit dozens of files.

## The Solution

Define semantic CSS classes in app.css that components reference:

```css
/* v2/assets/css/app.css */

/* Form Labels */
.gf-label {
  display: block;
  font-size: 1rem;        /* text-base */
  font-weight: 500;       /* font-medium */
  color: #374151;         /* text-gray-700 */
  margin-bottom: 0.25rem; /* mb-1 */
}

.gf-label-inline {
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 500;
  color: #374151;
  /* no margin - for inline labels like checkboxes */
}
```

## Tasks

1. **Create CSS classes in app.css:**
   - .gf-label (block labels for inputs)
   - .gf-label-inline (inline labels for checkboxes/toggles)
   - Any other label variants identified in audit

2. **Update core_components.ex:**
   - Change hardcoded 'text-base font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1' to 'gf-label'
   - Update all input types (text, select, textarea, checkbox)

3. **Update components/form_components.ex:**
   - Line 120 (multi_select): use .gf-label
   - Line 206 (field_wrapper): use .gf-label
   - Line 260 (toggle): use .gf-label-inline
   - Line 289 (radio_group): use .gf-label
   - Line 570 (multi_select_dropdown): use .gf-label

4. **Update admin/form_components.ex:**
   - Line 88 (alias_editor): use .gf-label

5. **Document the standard:**
   Add comment at top of app.css form section explaining the semantic classes

## Testing

1. Change .gf-label color in app.css temporarily
2. Verify the change appears on ALL form labels across the app
3. Revert the test change

## Dependencies

Depends on audit task (gallformers-46n0) which will identify all locations needing updates.

title: (EPIC) Implement OpenTofu for AWS Infrastructure
description:
Implement infrastructure-as-code using OpenTofu to manage all AWS resources.

## Background

Currently AWS resources are managed manually via console. This epic tracks implementing OpenTofu (open source Terraform fork under CNCF) to enable:
- Version controlled infrastructure changes
- Reproducible environment setup  
- Easier disaster recovery
- Cleanup of legacy cruft

## Decision: OpenTofu

**Why OpenTofu over alternatives:**
- Truly open source (MPL 2.0) under Linux Foundation/CNCF
- Self-hosted state in S3 (no vendor dependency)
- HCL syntax with huge ecosystem (3,900+ providers)
- Drop-in Terraform compatible
- Native S3 locking in v1.10 (no DynamoDB needed)

**Rejected alternatives:**
- Terraform: BSL license concerns (not open source since 2023)
- Pulumi: Vendor dependency unless self-hosting state
- CloudFormation: AWS-only, YAML verbose
- Ansible: Procedural, not declarative, poor state tracking

## Current AWS Resources (Account: 885187511538)

**S3 Buckets:**
| Bucket | Region | Access | Notes |
|--------|--------|--------|-------|
| `gallformers` | us-east-2 | Public read | Images, needs migration to us-east-1 |
| `gallformers-dev` | us-east-2 | Private | Dev images, consider deprecating |
| `gallformers-backups` | us-east-1 | Mixed | Litestream + public sanitized snapshots |
| `gallformers-full-backups` | us-east-1 | Private | Full backups with PII |

**CloudFront:**
- Distribution ID: `E3B3XXYW8G4SB2`
- Domain: `dhz6u1p7t6okk.cloudfront.net`
- Origin: `gallformers.s3.amazonaws.com`

**IAM Users:**
| User | Purpose | Policies |
|------|---------|----------|
| `jeff` | Admin/billing | `AWSBillingReadOnlyAccess` |
| `litestream-gallformers` | DB backups | `LitestreamGallformersBackup` (v3) |
| `s3-upload` | Image uploads | `s3-put` (v2) + inline `GallfomersImagesPolicy` |

**IAM Policy Details:**

`s3-put` policy (v2, updated 2026-01-20):
```json
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "s3:Put*",
                "s3:DeleteObject"
            ],
            "Resource": [
```

title: Bootstrap Phoenix Framework infrastructure
description:
Set up foundational infrastructure for Phoenix/Elixir-based V2, as an alternative to Go + HTMX.

## Purpose
Evaluate Phoenix Framework as a potential stack for V2. Phoenix LiveView provides real-time, server-rendered UI similar to HTMX but integrated into the framework.

## Why Consider Phoenix
- LiveView: Server-rendered real-time UI without writing JavaScript
- Built-in WebSocket support for live updates
- Excellent developer experience (hot reload, error pages)
- Strong conventions reduce decision fatigue
- Ecto for database access (similar to Prisma but more mature)
- Battle-tested at scale (Discord, Heroku dashboard)

## Scope

### 1. Phoenix Project Setup
- Install Elixir/Erlang (asdf or similar)
- Create new Phoenix project in v2-phoenix/ (or separate repo)
- Configure for SQLite (phoenix_sqlite3 adapter)
- Verify mix phx.server starts

### 2. Database Connection
- Configure Ecto repo for existing SQLite database
- Create read-only schemas for glossary table
- Verify can query glossary entries

### 3. LiveView Setup
- Confirm LiveView is enabled (default in Phoenix 1.7+)
- Create basic LiveView page to verify it works
- Understand mount/render/handle_event lifecycle

### 4. Tailwind Integration
- Phoenix 1.7+ includes Tailwind by default
- Port custom colors (gf-maroon, gf-sky-blue, etc.) to tailwind.config.js
- Verify styles compile

### 5. Base Layout
- Update root.html.heex with site structure
- Create app.html.heex layout matching current V2 design
- Header with logo/nav
- Footer

### 6. Deployment Research
- Evaluate Fly.io for Phoenix (well supported)
- Document deployment approach
- Note any cost differences from Go

### 7. Development Workflow
- Document setup steps
- mix phx.server for dev
- Create v2-phoenix/README.md or CLAUDE.md

## Deliverables
1. Working Phoenix project that starts
2. Can query SQLite database
3. Base layout matching current design
4. LiveView working on test route
5. Deployment plan documented

## Out of Scope
- Converting real pages (tracer bullet)
- Admin functionality
- Authentication
- Production deployment (just research)
close_reason: Phoenix infrastructure bootstrapped: project created, SQLite connected, LiveView working, base layout matches V2 design, glossary page implemented as proof of concept

title: Move all V1 code into v1/ subdirectory
description:
# Goal

Reorganize the repository so that all V1 (Next.js) code lives in a `v1/` subdirectory, leaving only docs and v2 at the root. This creates a cleaner separation as we transition to the Phoenix/LiveView rewrite.

# What Moves to v1/

## Directories (16 total)
- `__tests__/`
- `components/`
- `hooks/`
- `lambdas/`
- `layouts/`
- `libs/`
- `migrations/`
- `nginx-conf/`
- `pages/`
- `prisma/`
- `public/`
- `ref/`
- `scripts/`
- `services/`
- `types/`
- `usda_plants/`

## Config/Build Files
- `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `yarn.lock`
- `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.ci.json`, `tsconfig.ci.tsbuildinfo`
- `next.config.mjs`, `next-env.d.ts`
- `jest.config.js`, `jest.setup.ts`, `jest.polyfills.js`
- `eslint.config.js`, `.eslintrc.json`, `.lintstagedrc.json`
- `jsconfig.json`
- `.babelrc`, `.prettierrc.cjs`
- `dbsetup.js`, `migrate.js`

## Docker/Deploy Files
- `Dockerfile`, `Dockerfile.fly`, `gallformers.Dockerfile`
- `docker-compose.yml`
- `fly.toml`
- `Makefile`
- `maintenance.html`

## Environment Files
- `.env.development`
- `.env.local-docker`
- `.env.local.example`
- `.env.production`
- `.dockerignore`
- `.nvmrc`

# What Stays at Root

- `.beads/` - Issue tracking
- `.claude/` - Claude Code config
- `.git/` - Git data
- `.github/` - CI workflows (will need path updates)
- `.husky/` - Git hooks
- `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `LICENSE`
- `openspec/` - Spec system
- `runbooks/` - Operational docs
- `prompts/` - AI prompts
- `research/` - Research docs
- `v2/` - Phoenix/LiveView rewrite
- `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes`, `.gitconfig`
- `.copilotignore`, `.cursorignore`, `.cursorrules`

# Implementation Steps

1. **Create v1/ and move files**
   ```bash
   mkdir v1
   git mv __tests__ components hooks lambdas layouts libs migrations nginx-conf pages prisma public ref scripts services types usda_plants v1/
   git mv package.json package-lock.json yarn.lock v1/
   git mv tsconfig.json tsconfig.ci.json tsconfig.ci.tsbuildinfo v1/
   git mv next.config.mjs next-env.d.ts v1/
   git mv jest.config.js jest.setup.ts jest.polyfills.js v1/
   git mv eslint.config.js .eslintrc.json .lintstagedrc.json v1/
   git mv jsconfig.json .babelrc .prettierrc.cjs v1/
   git mv dbsetup.js migrate.js v1/
   git mv Dockerfile Dockerfile.fly gallformers.Dockerfile v1/
   git mv docker-compose.yml fly.toml Makefile v1/
   git mv maintenance.html v1/
   git mv .env.development .env.local-docker .env.local.example .env.production v1/
   git mv .dockerignore .nvmrc v1/
   ```

2. **Update paths in moved files**
   - `v1/Makefile` - Update any paths
   - `v1/Dockerfile*` - Update COPY/WORKDIR paths
   - `v1/docker-compose.yml` - Update volume mounts and build context
   - `v1/fly.toml` - Verify paths still work

3. **Update CI workflows in .github/workflows/**
   - Change working directory to `v1/` for V1 build/test/deploy steps
   - Update any path references

4. **Update root documentation**
   - `CLAUDE.md` - Update project structure section and paths
   - `README.md` - Update setup instructions to reference v1/

5. **Update .gitignore at root**
   - May need to adjust paths or create v1/.gitignore

6. **Test V1 still works**
   ```bash
   cd v1
   yarn install
   yarn lint
   yarn check-types
   yarn test
   yarn build
   ```

7. **Test Docker build**
   ```bash
   cd v1
   make build
   ```

# Git History Note

Git history is preserved with `git mv`. Use `git log --follow <path>` to trace history across the move.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All V1 code is in v1/ subdirectory
- [ ] V1 builds successfully from v1/ directory
- [ ] V1 tests pass
- [ ] Docker build works
- [ ] CI workflows updated and passing
- [ ] Documentation updated to reflect new structure
- [ ] No broken imports or path references

title: Fix checkbox hidden input in all component files
description:
Multiple components with checkboxes are missing the hidden input pattern, causing checkboxes to not properly toggle to false.

## The Problem

HTML checkboxes only send a value when checked. When unchecked, they send nothing, so the server keeps the old value. The fix is to include a hidden input with value='false' before the checkbox.

## Correct Pattern (from core_components.ex)

```heex
<input type="hidden" name={@name} value="false" />
<input type="checkbox" name={@name} value="true" checked={@checked} />
```

## Components That Need Fixing

1. **components/form_components.ex - rename_modal** (lines 759-764)
   - Has raw checkbox without hidden input
   - Note: This may be deleted if duplicate removal task consolidates to admin version

2. **admin/form_components.ex - rename_modal** (lines 245-250)
   - Has raw checkbox without hidden input

3. **Any other components with checkboxes** - audit during fix

## Components That Are Correct

- core_components.ex <.input type="checkbox"> - has hidden input ✓
- form_components.ex <.toggle> - has hidden input ✓

## Tasks

1. Search all component files for type="checkbox"
2. For each checkbox found, verify hidden input exists
3. Add hidden input where missing
4. Test each affected component

## Testing

For each fixed checkbox:
1. Load the page/modal with checkbox unchecked
2. Check the checkbox, verify it stays checked
3. Uncheck the checkbox, verify it stays unchecked
4. Submit/save and reload, verify state persisted correctly

title: Expand FormHelpers behaviour with default implementations
description:
Expand v2/lib/gallformers_web/live/admin/form_helpers.ex with default implementations for common patterns.

## Current State

FormHelpers provides dirty state tracking (init_form_state, mark_dirty, reset_dirty, handle_form_event, discard_confirm_modal). All 8 admin forms use it.

## New Callbacks to Add (with default implementations)

**Required (no defaults):**
- context_module/0 - returns context module (e.g., Gallformers.Glossary)
- entity_key/0 - returns assign key (e.g., :entry)
- list_path/0 - returns list page path for navigation

**Optional (with defaults):**
- load_entity/1 - default: context_module().get!(id)
- entity_label/0 - default: derive from entity_key
- prepare_params/1 - default: pass through unchanged
- after_create/2 - default: flash + push_navigate to list_path
- after_update/2 - default: flash + push_navigate to list_path

## New Helper Functions to Inject

**init_admin_form(socket, session)**
Standard mount setup: assign current_user, page_title, call init_form_state.

**apply_new_action(socket, extra_assigns \\ [])**
Create empty struct, changeset, assign form/mode/:new, merge extra_assigns.

**apply_edit_action(socket, id, extra_assigns \\ [])**
Load entity, handle nil (flash + redirect), changeset, assign form/mode/:edit.

**handle_validate(params, socket)**
Standard validate: change_entity, set :action, assign form, mark_dirty.

**handle_save(params, socket)**
Dispatch to create or update based on mode, call prepare_params, handle result.

## Reference

Compare any two simple forms (e.g., glossary_live/form.ex and place_live/form.ex) to see the duplicated patterns.

## Backward Compatibility

Existing forms should continue to work. New helpers are opt-in - forms can call them from their handle_event/mount or continue with custom logic.
close_reason: Expanded FormHelpers with CRUD helpers including behaviour callbacks (context_module, entity_key, list_path, form_key, entity_label, entity_struct, new_entity, load_entity, change_entity, create_entity, update_entity, prepare_params, after_create, after_update) and helper functions (init_admin_form, apply_new_action, apply_edit_action, handle_validate, handle_save). All helpers are opt-in via crud_helpers: true option to maintain backward compatibility.

title: Form Component Consistency & Consolidation
description:
Consolidate form components across core_components.ex, components/form_components.ex, and admin/form_components.ex to achieve visual and behavioral consistency across the entire app.

## Problem Statement

We have ~1900 lines of component code across 3 files, but ~80% of form elements use raw HTML instead. This has caused:
- Visual inconsistency (admin uses text-sm, public uses text-base for labels)
- Behavioral bugs (checkboxes missing hidden input pattern)
- Code duplication (rename_modal exists in 2 files)
- Maintenance burden (changes must be made in many places)
- **No centralized styling** (changing a style requires editing dozens of files)

## Current State

**Component Files:**
- core_components.ex: Core primitives (<.input>, etc.) - uses text-base, has proper checkbox handling
- components/form_components.ex: Extended components (btn, multi_select, search_input, toggle, etc.)
- admin/form_components.ex: Admin-specific (form_actions, alias_editor, rename_modal)

**Raw HTML Usage (131 instances across 24 files):**
- Text inputs: 29
- Selects: 21  
- Textareas: 7
- Buttons: 60
- Checkboxes: 14

**Component Usage:** Only 35 instances

## Goals

1. **Visual consistency** - all form elements look the same across admin and public pages
2. **Behavioral consistency** - all elements work correctly (e.g., checkboxes toggle properly)
3. **Single source of truth for components** - one canonical component for each element type
4. **Single source of truth for styling** - semantic CSS classes in app.css that components reference
5. **Admin layering** - admin-specific components build ON TOP of shared foundation

## Key Principle: Centralized Styling

All form styling should be defined in app.css as semantic CSS classes:
```css
.gf-label { /* label styling - change here, changes everywhere */ }
.gf-input { /* input styling */ }
.gf-select { /* select styling */ }
.gf-checkbox { /* already exists */ }
.gf-btn-primary { /* button styling */ }
```

Components reference these classes instead of hardcoding Tailwind. This allows global style changes from ONE location.

## Success Criteria

- All raw HTML form elements replaced with components
- No duplicate components across files
- Consistent styling via semantic CSS classes in app.css
- All checkboxes include hidden input for proper false value handling
- Changing .gf-label in app.css changes ALL labels across the app

---

## Agent Coordination Prompt

Use this prompt to coordinate agents working on this epic:

```

**Setup:**
- Integration branch: adopt-phoenix-liveview
- Worktrees available: ~/dev/gallformers-code1, ~/dev/gallformers-code2
- Max parallel streams: 2 (code1 and code2)

**For each batch of work:**

1. Run `bd ready` to see available work
2. Identify up to 2 tasks that can be worked in parallel (no dependency conflicts)
3. Generate prompts for each task (see template below)
4. Wait for user approval before proceeding to next batch

**Task Types:**

- **Research/Design tasks** (audit, proposals, decisions): Agent MUST involve user in decision-making. Do not create docs or make architectural decisions without user input. Present findings and recommendations, then wait for user direction.

- **Code tasks** (migrations, fixes, implementations): Agent can work independently, following the bead description. Run precommit checks before completing.

**Prompt Template for Code Tasks:**

```
Work bead [BEAD-ID]: [TITLE]

**Setup:**

title: SQL injection risk in updateExistingSpecies - use parameterized queries
description:
In libs/db/taxonomy.ts:628-642, the updateExistingSpecies() function directly interpolates g.name and g.id into SQL strings without parameterization.

**Current code:**
```typescript
const sql = `UPDATE species
    SET name = [REPLACE](name, SUBSTRING(name, 1, INSTR(name, ' ') - 1), '${g.name}')
    WHERE id IN (... WHERE t.id = ${g.id} ...)`;
return db.$executeRaw(Prisma.sql([sql]));
```

**Risk:** Low - endpoint requires Auth0 authentication (admin-only), but violates defense-in-depth.

**Fix:** Use Prisma's tagged template literal for parameterized queries:
```typescript
return db.$executeRaw`UPDATE species SET name = ... WHERE ...`;
```

Note: The REPLACE logic itself is correct and handles edge cases properly.
